body:
This PR: 

* adds a `NoPenetration` boundary condition, addressing #328.
* converts some of the boundary condition `struct`s to `NamedTuple`s, where appropriate, paving the way for more generalization / code reduction
* overhauls the `fill_halo_regions!` function to dispatch on boundary condition type, partially addressing #307 
* fixes bugs in the halo filling algorithm, addressing #328

What's left to do

- [ ] use halo regions for z boundary conditions, eliminating `if`-statements once and for all in operators and paving the way for a triply periodic model
- [x] convert turbulence closure operators / diffusivities to use `OffsetArrays` / halo regions
title: Halo regions + boundary conditions overhaul and bug fixes

body:
PR co-authored with @sandreza 

This PR tests Oceananigans with the Pearson vortex test from p. 310 of "Nodal Discontinuous Galerkin Methods: Algorithms, Analysis, and Application" by Hesthaven & Warburton.

Error after 100 time steps:
```julia
i: 100, t: 0.008, Δu: (avg=0.000224, max=0.000224), Δw: (avg=0.000224, max=0.000224)
```
which compares well with the DG error (considering our low-order numerics).

We discovered that recomputing w from continuity breaks this test because du/dx = 0 and dv/dy = 0 by construction so w = 0 even though this is wrong. This is probably because we need to impose Neumann boundary condition on w, but recomputing w from continuity requires us to assume the value of `w[i, j, :]`, which we take to be 0 since we usually run with a rigid lid. So we switched to explicitly time stepping w which means this PR is "DO NOT MERGE" is its current state.

Note: Before the test is merged, more comments are needed in the pearson vortex test.

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

title: Pearson vortex test

body:
This PR uses a GPUifyLoops kernel to set initial conditions so `set_ic!` runs fast on GPUs.

Should make life more comfortable.

cc @sandreza

body:
Don't need JLD or HDF5 since PR #329 removed the last use of a JLD output writer.

This should speed up CI testing by quite a bit.
title: Remove JLD and HDF5 dependency
